name: .22 Long Rifle
description: The world's most popular cartridge. Ubiquitous for plinking, small game, training, and rimfire competition.

name: .22 WMR
description: Rimfire magnum for small game and varmints. Significantly more reach and energy than .22 LR.

name: .17 HMR
description: High-velocity rimfire for varmints and precision small-game hunting. Flat-shooting with minimal recoil.

name: .17 WSM
description: The fastest and most powerful rimfire cartridge commercially available. Based on a .27-caliber nail gun blank.

name: .204 Ruger
description: Ultra-high-velocity varmint cartridge. Extremely flat trajectory with minimal recoil, popular for prairie dog shooting.

name: .220 Swift
description: The first factory cartridge to exceed 4,000 fps. A classic varmint round that remains popular among long-range varmint shooters.

name: .22-250 Remington
description: One of the most popular varmint cartridges ever made. Excellent velocity and accuracy for predator and prairie dog hunting.

name: .224 Valkyrie
description: Designed for long-range shooting from the AR-15 platform. Uses high-BC .224 bullets for supersonic performance past 1,000 yards.

name: .25 Creedmoor
description: Necked-down 6.5 Creedmoor for .25-caliber bullets. Flat-shooting medium-game cartridge with excellent long-range ballistics. SAAMI-accepted in 2025.

name: .257 Roberts
description: Classic medium-game cartridge with mild recoil. A favorite of handloaders and a versatile deer/antelope round.

name: .257 Weatherby Magnum
description: The fastest commercial .25-caliber cartridge. A Weatherby classic that delivers blistering velocity for open-country hunting.

name: .25-06 Remington
description: Popular long-range varmint and medium-game cartridge. Necked-down .30-06 offering flat trajectory with moderate recoil.

name: 6mm BR Remington
description: Benchrest-born cartridge renowned for inherent accuracy. Parent case for the 6mm Dasher and 6BRA competition cartridges.

name: 6mm BRA
description: Ackley Improved version of the 6mm BR with a 40-degree shoulder for slightly more capacity. Very popular in PRS/NRL competition.

name: 6mm XC
description: Designed by David Tubb for across-the-course NRA High Power competition. Optimized for heavy 6mm bullets at moderate velocity.

name: 6mm Remington
description: Necked-down .257 Roberts that preceded the .243 Winchester. Slightly more case capacity than the .243 but less commercially popular.

name: 6x45mm
description: The .223 Remington necked up to 6mm. An AR-15-compatible cartridge offering heavier bullets and more energy than .223.

name: .264 Winchester Magnum
description: An ahead-of-its-time 6.5mm magnum that suffered from barrel-burning reputation. Has experienced a modest revival among handloaders.

name: 6.5 Grendel
description: AR-15-compatible cartridge designed for long-range performance. Uses high-BC 6.5mm bullets from the AR-15 platform.

name: 6.5 Weatherby RPM
description: Weatherby's modern rebated-rim magnum designed for standard-length actions. Bridges the gap between 6.5 Creedmoor and 6.5 PRC.

name: .26 Nosler
description: The fastest SAAMI-standardized 6.5mm cartridge. Designed for extreme long-range hunting with blistering velocity.

name: 6.8 SPC
description: Developed for the U.S. military as a 5.56 NATO replacement candidate. Offers significant energy improvement over 5.56 in the AR-15 platform.

name: 6.8 Western
description: Winchester/Browning collaboration designed for heavy, high-BC .277 bullets. A modern short-action cartridge for Western big game.

name: .270 WSM
description: Short-action .270 magnum that nearly matches .270 Weatherby Magnum performance in a shorter, stiffer action.

name: .270 Weatherby Magnum
description: Roy Weatherby's classic high-velocity .270. Faster than the .270 Win with more reach for open-country hunting.

name: .277 Fury
description: Developed by SIG Sauer for the U.S. Army NGSW program. Uses a hybrid-case design rated to 80,000 PSI — the highest MAP of any SAAMI cartridge.

name: 7x57mm Mauser
description: One of the most influential cartridges in history. Mild recoil with excellent killing power — still popular in Europe and Africa for medium game.

name: .280 Remington
description: A .30-06-length 7mm cartridge that rivals the 7mm Rem Mag with less recoil. Underappreciated but excellent for all-around big game.

name: .280 Ackley Improved
description: SAAMI-standardized Ackley Improved version of the .280 Remington. A top-tier Western big game cartridge that nearly matches 7mm Rem Mag ballistics.

name: .284 Winchester
description: Short-action 7mm with a fat, rebated-rim case. Commercially unsuccessful but its case became the basis for the 6.5-284 and other competition wildcats.

name: .28 Nosler
description: High-velocity 7mm magnum from Nosler. Offers significant velocity advantage over 7mm Rem Mag for long-range hunting.

name: 7mm WSM
description: Short-action 7mm magnum from Winchester's WSM family. Near-7mm Rem Mag performance in a short action.

name: 7mm Backcountry
description: SAAMI-accepted in 2025 with an 80,000 PSI MAP. A modern high-pressure short-action 7mm designed for lightweight mountain rifles.

name: 7mm SAUM
description: Remington's short-action 7mm ultra magnum. Gained a strong following in PRS/NRL competition for its excellent case design.

name: .30-30 Winchester
description: America's deer cartridge. The quintessential lever-action round that has taken more whitetail deer than any other centerfire cartridge.

name: .300 Savage
description: Parent case of the .308 Winchester. A short-action .30-caliber that delivered near-.30-06 performance in its era.

name: .300 HAM'R
description: Wilson Combat's AR-15-compatible .30-caliber cartridge. More energy than .300 Blackout with better range using conventional bullets.

name: .300 RUM
description: One of the most powerful .30-caliber magnums available. Extreme velocity and energy for long-range hunting and ELR shooting.

name: .300 Norma Magnum
description: Adopted by U.S. SOCOM as their Advanced Sniper Rifle cartridge. A .338 Norma Mag case necked to .30 cal for extreme long-range precision.

name: .30 Nosler
description: Nosler's .30-caliber magnum that matches or exceeds .300 Win Mag performance in a standard-length action without a belt.

name: .308 Marlin Express
description: Lever-action-friendly .30-caliber delivering near-.308 Win performance from tubular-magazine rifles with flex-tip bullets.

name: .300 Weatherby Magnum
description: Roy Weatherby's flagship cartridge and the company's most popular round. A powerful .30-caliber magnum for long-range big game.

name: .338 Federal
description: A .308 Winchester case necked up to .338. Offers heavy-bullet performance for large game in a short-action, lightweight rifle.

name: .338 Norma Magnum
description: Designed to launch 300gr .338 bullets from a shorter action than .338 Lapua Mag. Adopted by SOCOM and gaining traction in ELR competition.

name: .338 RUM
description: Remington's .338 ultra magnum offering more case capacity than the .338 Lapua in a rebated-rim design.

name: .340 Weatherby Magnum
description: Weatherby's .33-caliber magnum. More powerful than .338 Win Mag with the signature Weatherby double-radius shoulder.

name: .338-378 Weatherby Magnum
description: One of the most powerful .338 cartridges ever produced. Extreme velocity and energy for the largest game and ELR shooting.

name: .350 Legend
description: Winchester's straight-wall cartridge designed for deer hunting in states that restrict bottleneck cartridges. AR-15 compatible.

name: .35 Whelen
description: A .30-06 case necked up to .35 caliber. A classic for heavy timber hunting with large, heavy bullets for elk and moose.

name: .375 H&H Magnum
description: The most versatile dangerous-game cartridge ever designed. The minimum legal caliber for dangerous game in most of Africa.

name: .375 Ruger
description: Modern rimless alternative to the .375 H&H that matches its ballistics in a standard-length action without a belt.

name: .375 CheyTac
description: Purpose-built ELR cartridge gaining dominance in Extreme Long Range competition. Superior BC bullets for 2,000+ yard shooting.

name: .408 CheyTac
description: Extreme long-range military and competition cartridge. Designed for precision engagements beyond 2,000 yards.

name: .416 Rigby
description: The gold standard of dangerous-game cartridges. Low operating pressure ensures reliable extraction in extreme heat.

name: .416 Remington Magnum
description: A .375 H&H-length .416 that matches .416 Rigby ballistics in a standard magnum action. Popular for African dangerous game.

name: .416 Barrett
description: ELR cartridge based on the .50 BMG case necked down to .416. Designed by Barrett for extreme long-range precision.

name: .45-70 Government
description: America's oldest centerfire cartridge still in common use. A lever-action classic that's had a massive resurgence for brush hunting and guide guns.

name: .450 Bushmaster
description: Thumper-class AR-15 cartridge designed for big-bore performance. Popular in straight-wall-only deer hunting states.

name: .458 SOCOM
description: Big-bore AR-15 cartridge developed after the 1993 Battle of Mogadishu. Maximum stopping power from the AR-15 platform.

name: .458 Winchester Magnum
description: Winchester's dangerous-game cartridge for African big five. The most commercially successful .458-caliber cartridge.

name: .458 Lott
description: An improved .458 Win Mag with a longer case. The gold standard for cape buffalo and elephant among professional hunters.

name: .50 BMG
description: The original extreme long-range cartridge. Military machine-gun round adapted for precision ELR shooting and anti-materiel use.

name: .50 Beowulf
description: Alexander Arms big-bore AR-15 cartridge. Maximum energy at close range for home defense and hog hunting.

name: 7.62x39mm
description: The AK-47 cartridge. One of the most produced cartridges in history, widely available and effective for medium game at moderate range.

name: 5.45x39mm
description: The Soviet/Russian military small-bore cartridge developed for the AK-74. High velocity with tumbling characteristics.

name: 7.62x54mmR
description: The oldest military cartridge still in frontline service. The Russian equivalent of the .30-06, used in Mosin-Nagant and SVD/Dragunov rifles.

name: 8x57mm Mauser
description: Germany's primary military cartridge through two world wars. Still widely used in European sporting rifles.

name: 9.3x62mm Mauser
description: Popular European and African medium-bore cartridge. Legal for all African game in many countries and a favorite of European hunters.

name: 9.3x74mmR
description: Rimmed version of the 9.3mm bore for break-action and double rifles. Popular for European driven hunts and African safari rifles.

name: .25 Weatherby RPM
description: Weatherby's necked-down 6.5 RPM for .25-caliber bullets. More capacity than the .25 Creedmoor for higher velocity with heavy .25-cal bullets.

name: .22 Nosler
description: Nosler's AR-15-compatible .22-caliber cartridge offering significant velocity gains over .223 Rem with minimal modifications.

name: .300 RSAUM
description: Remington's short-action .30-caliber ultra magnum. Offers near-.300 Win Mag performance in a short action with excellent accuracy potential.

name: 6.5 SAUM
description: A wildcat/semi-commercial necked-down .300 SAUM. Offers significant velocity over 6.5 Creedmoor in a short action, popular in PRS circles.

name: .22 Creedmoor
description: Wildcat necked-down 6.5 Creedmoor to .22 caliber. Ultra-high velocity for varminting and long-range target shooting.

name: .300 Ruger Compact Magnum
description: A short-action .30-caliber magnum from Hornady and Ruger. Near-.300 Win Mag velocities from short barrels in compact rifles.

name: .325 WSM
description: Winchester's 8mm entry in the short magnum family. The only modern American 8mm commercial cartridge — comparable to the 8x68mm S.

name: .240 Weatherby Magnum
description: The fastest commercial 6mm cartridge. Weatherby's varmint/medium-game magnum delivering over 4,000 fps with light bullets.

name: .17 Hornet
description: A necked-down .22 Hornet for .17-caliber centerfire performance at rimfire noise levels. Ideal for pest control in noise-sensitive areas.

name: .22 Hornet
description: A classic small centerfire varmint cartridge. Low noise and recoil make it ideal for pest control and small game.

name: .222 Remington
description: The original benchrest cartridge that dominated accuracy competition for decades. Predecessor to the .223 Remington.

name: .222 Remington Magnum
description: An intermediate step between the .222 Rem and .223 Rem. Now largely obsolete but historically significant as a failed military candidate.

name: .223 WSSM
description: Winchester's super short magnum .22 centerfire. Extremely high velocity from a fat, short case — limited commercial success.

name: .243 WSSM
description: The 6mm version of Winchester's super short magnum family. Highest velocity of any 6mm commercial cartridge.

name: 7.62x40mm WT
description: Wilson Combat's .30-caliber AR-15 cartridge. Uses .223 brass trimmed and necked up — similar concept to .300 Blackout but with a different case design.

name: .260 Ackley Improved
description: Ackley Improved version of the .260 Remington with a 40-degree shoulder. Modest velocity gain and improved brass life over the parent case.

name: 6mm-284
description: A .284 Winchester case necked to 6mm. Popular in 600- and 1,000-yard benchrest competition for its accuracy and velocity.

name: .30 Remington AR
description: Remington's .30-caliber AR-15 cartridge offering 7.62x39mm ballistics with better accuracy and AR-15 bolt-face compatibility.

name: .375 EnABELR
description: Applied Ballistics' purpose-designed ELR cartridge. Optimized for 2,000+ yard competition with custom high-BC .375 projectiles.

name: .338 EnABELR
description: Applied Ballistics' .338-caliber ELR competition cartridge. Designed alongside the .375 EnABELR for extreme distance shooting.
